description: Manoto (Me and You) was a Persian-language satellite television channel founded in 2010 by [Kayvan Abbassi](/people/kayvan-abbassi) and his wife Marjan Abbassi, an Iranian-British couple, and headquartered in London. Privately funded with no confirmed state backing, Manoto became one of the most-watched Persian-language channels globally, combining entertainment programming with news and current affairs coverage. The channel maintained a secular, anti-regime editorial line and attracted a broad audience inside Iran despite satellite jamming. The Iranian regime alleged MI6 and Mossad connections to the channel — claims that remain unsubstantiated. In 2024 Manoto ceased live broadcasting and shifted to on-demand digital content.
editorial_notes: Ceased live broadcasting 2024. Founded by Kayvan and Marjan Abbassi. Privately funded.
